D3 Chemistry L6 Create Reference

Diamond NV Centre — Single-Atom Quantum Defect

a nitrogen atom + adjacent carbon vacancy in diamond — the NV⁻ centre absorbs 532 nm green and emits 637 nm red; each centre is a single-spin qubit usable at room temperature

The NV centre is a point defect with rich physics: a substitutional N adjacent to a C vacancy in the diamond lattice. The negatively-charged NV⁻ has a singlet ground state with three sub-levels at zero magnetic field; under 532 nm green excitation it relaxes to 637 nm red phonon-assisted fluorescence. The spin state can be read optically — the centre is one of the few room-temperature quantum sensors.

Drag the green excitation power. Emitted red intensity rises linearly until saturation. The 532-nm-in / 637-nm-out wavelength labels reveal the Stokes shift.
